options: I am excited about this news.

III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 On May 24, 2022, the news of mass shooting in an elementary school of Texas, United States, shocked the whole world. According to the police investigation, the 18-year-old shooter 21 Robb Elementary School before lunchtime and started shooting door by door. Although he was shot dead by the police, 19 kids and 2 adults unfortunately died from this shooting. Many others were injured while running away. Some of the students 22 in the incident suffered from the trauma after the accident. The parents of the victims were 23 shocked 23 accept the consequences. They wished they could have taught their kids how to protect themselves from gun violence. In order to 24 people’s awareness of children’s rights, thousands of people took to the streets to push lawmakers to take action on gun control. Joe Biden, the U.S. president, called for stricter gun control than before. Donald Trump, the former U.S. president, even suggested that teachers in the U.S. 25 equipped with a gun at schools for the purpose of protecting their armless kids.
questions.explanation: 鄰居:我好幾天都沒看到你媽媽。她最近都去哪了? 他的錯誤行為道歉了,但仍堅持認為他的意圖是為了保 艾咪:她正忙於照顧我奶奶。她們現在在醫院。 護他的家人。之後,儘管威爾沒有被要回奧斯卡獎座, 鄰居:哦, 如果需要幫助,請讓我 但在往後十年之內,他不會再被邀請出席典禮。至於克 知道。 里斯,他 (28) 被視為一位缺乏說話藝術的受害者。到目前 艾咪:謝謝你的好意。 為止,雙方原先的立場仍未改變。威爾的粉絲強烈地支 (A) 我對這個消息感到很興奮。 持他的保護行為; (29) 而克里斯的追隨者對開惡劣玩笑話 (B) 我對你的想法沒興趣。 並不以為意。從這次事件中,我們可從中學得一些教訓。 (C) 很遺憾聽到這個消息。 一是,即使我們試圖展現幽默,我們也應尊重他人。此 (D) 我等不及想見你了。 外,我們應該要知道保護行為並不 (30) 等於使用暴力。換 句話說,在我們在反應不滿之前,最好三思而後行。 III. 綜合測驗

options: have ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 26-30 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 th At the 94 Academy Awards, Will Smith went on the stage and then slapped the host, Chris Rock, across the face out of a sudden. The shocking attack 26 live to the whole world. Later on, it caused a lot of discussions. Some people criticized Will’s violence; 27 looked down on Chris’s joke about his wife’s bald head. Will apologized for his wrongdoing but he still insisted that his intention was to protect his family. Afterward, although Will was not asked to return the Oscar trophy, he would not be invited to the ceremony in the following ten years. As for Chris, he was 28 as a victim who lacked language arts. Until now, the positions of both sides still remain unchanged. Will’s fans strongly stood up for his protective behaviors 29 Chris’s followers did not feel bad about making mean jokes. From this event, we can learn some lessons. One is that we should show respect for others even when we try to be humorous. Besides, we should know that protective behaviors are not equal 30 using violence. In other words, we had better think twice before we react to something unpleasant.

Read the report card of one student and answer the questions. REPORT CARD NAME: LING CHENG Course: General English (Grade 10) DATE: June 20, 2022 TERM: 2022 Semester 2 STUDENT’S STRENGTHS ‧ Personality: outgoing, friendly, humorous ‧ Performances: (1) active participation (2) creative hands-on crafts AREAS FOR IMPROVEMENT SUGGESTIONS ‧ Reading Comprehension Skills Ling can... ‧ Critical Thinking Skills (1) read picture books 30 minutes per day (2) train typing speed 30 minutes per day ‧ Technology Application Skills
questions.explanation: 下列何者敘述正確? 學博覽會,因此您可以在校園裡我們所設的攤位跟我們 (A) 約翰幾乎不知道開銷控管。 連繫獲取更詳細的資訊。屆時若您有任何的問題,我們 (B) 約翰規劃了一個愉快的自駕旅行。 都很樂意回答。然而您若是未克蒞臨我們的攤位,強烈 (C) 約翰很有可能買了些禮物回家鄉。 建議您上我們的網站獲取更多資訊。或者,您也可以透 (D) 約翰旅遊時花最少錢在住宿上。 過郵件來信 (service2022@gmail.com) 向我們索取紙本印 Question 33-34 刷品。 李老師是一名班導師。她為每位學生寫了一些成績 單評語。閱讀一名學生的成績單並回答問題。 期待與您相見。 成績單 姓名:程玲 課程:普通英文 ( 高一 ) 李梅 日期: 2022 年 6 月 20 日 學期: 2022 第 2 學期 學生的優勢 ‧個性:外向的、友善的、幽默的 ‧表現: (1) 高度參與 (2) 手藝作品具創意 進步的空間 建議 ‧閱讀技巧 玲可以... ‧批判思考技巧 (1) 閱讀繪本每日 30 分鐘 ‧科技應用技巧 (2) 訓練打字速度每日 30 分鐘 教師的話 教導玲是一件很開心的事。這學期整體來看,玲表現良 好。課堂間她總是待人有禮,樂於助人。同學們及老師 都非常喜愛她。然而,總是還有可以更進步的地方。她 似乎傾向快速地完成工作,所以她可能有時會造成一些 無心之過。當她將步調放慢下來時,謹慎小心地完成工 作,她就可以把工作做得很好。未來,我會想看到她的

Ling always does the work not only well but also quickly. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 35-37 題 To: All Grade 12 students From: International Education Institute th Date: May 18, 2022 (Wednesday) Dear Grade 12 students, Hope this letter finds you well. Before you go to college, I believe you must need more educational resources for planning your coming future. If you consider getting your bachelor’s degree in Australia, we can help! Our main office is near the school gate of Taipei University. You can visit us during weekdays from 9 (A)m. to 5 p.m. For your convenience, we will take part in the International University Exposition held in your school next Wednesday. You can reach us for more details in our booth on campus. Then if you have any questions, we are pleased to answer them. But if you don’t have time to visit our booth, it is strongly suggested that you visit our website for more information. Or, you can ask for hard copies by shooting us an email (service2022@gmail.com). Look forward to seeing you. May Li

To ask for hard copies via email. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 In the history of civilization, humans have spared no effort to fight for their rights but have hardly given “human rights” a clear definition. In general, human rights are entitled to every person without any conditions. That is, no matter where you come from, what you believe in, or how you live a life, you deserve to be treated as equally as any other person in this world. The notion of human rights appeared in 539 (B)(C) when the first king of Persia, Cyrus the Great, released slaves. As for its popularization, we could trace its history back to the time when people strived for legal civil rights. The Great Charter (1215) was made to remind people that even a king cannot change laws at will. Hundreds of years later, the Petition of Rights in 1628 officially stopped kings’ abuse of power. From then on, more people were allowed to fight for their civil liberties. However, to fulfill the fundamental human rights, the above was not enough. In the 1700s, the core of human rights turned into an emphasis on one’s freedom. The basic civil rights to organize government were announced and publicized officially in the Constitution of the U.S.(A) To perfect the notion of human rights, the Bill of Rights (1791) further guaranteed liberties to an individual, such as freedom of speech, religion, assembly, and petition*. Today, people not just seek for the equality of civil rights, but focus more on the rights for individual needs. For example, Human Rights Act (1998) laid down the law to prevent personality rights from being violated. Personal data, like financial condition, contact information, or even personal photos, should not be given away without permission. Besides, we still make efforts to reveal hidden unfairness happening between genders or in family relations. Perhaps, the definitions of human rights might continue changing with time. *petition 請願

(B) 有權勢的人仁慈地奴役窮人。 In order to / To take care of / look after his parents, Peter  [ 錯誤:第二段 Cyrus the Great, 539 B.C] decided to / determined to / was determined to move back (C) 一位父親命令他的女兒嫁給一個有錢人。 to his hometown.  [ 錯誤:第四段 gender equality, family relations] 評閱指標 (D) 一位律師透過列舉不公不義之事批評政府。 級分 說明
